Record Class AndHitbox2D

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.phys2d.impl.AndHitbox2D
Record Components:
children - the child hitboxes
All Implemented Interfaces:
Hitbox2D
public record AndHitbox2D(Collection<? extends Hitbox2D> children) extends Record implements Hitbox2D
An intersection of 2D hitboxes. This hitbox is finite only if at least one child hitbox is finite.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AndHitbox2D(Collection<? extends Hitbox2D> children)
    Creates an instance of a AndHitbox2D record class.
    AndHitbox2D(Hitbox2D... children)
    Array version of AndHitbox2D(Collection).

    Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox, if it's finite, otherwise returns an empty optional.

    Hitbox2D

    Creates a 2D hitbox that covers every point except those covered by this hitbox.

    optimize()
    Returns an AndHitbox2D with same children as this one, but without those that fully contain any other child (they're effectively redundant); if there are no children, returns NeverHitbox2D.INSTANCE.
    final String
    toString()
    Returns a string representation of this record class.

  • Constructor Details

    • AndHitbox2D

      public AndHitbox2D(Collection<? extends Hitbox2D> children)
      Creates an instance of a AndHitbox2D record class.
      Parameters:
      children - the value for the children record component

    • AndHitbox2D

      public AndHitbox2D(Hitbox2D... children)
      Array version of AndHitbox2D(Collection).
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if children is null

  • Method Details

    • optimize

      public Hitbox2D optimize()
      Returns an AndHitbox2D with same children as this one, but without those that fully contain any other child (they're effectively redundant); if there are no children, returns NeverHitbox2D.INSTANCE.
      Returns:
      an optimized version of this hitbox

    • intersects

      public boolean intersects(Hitbox2D other)
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Checks whether this hitbox intersects with the given one.

      Intersection is symmetric: a.intersects(b) == b.intersects(a)

      Specified by:
      intersects in interface Hitbox2D
      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox, must not be null
      Returns:
      whether the hitboxes overlap

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Hitbox2D other)
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Checks whether this hitbox fully contains the given one.

      Containment is not always symmetric, unlike intersection. It's symmetric only for equal hitboxes; otherwise, it's asymmetric.

      A finite hitbox can never contain an infinite one. If this hitbox and the given one are infinite, this method checks for equality instead.

      Specified by:
      contains in interface Hitbox2D
      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox, must not be null
      Returns:
      whether this hitbox fully contains the given one

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Point2D point)
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Checks whether the given point lies within this hitbox.
      Specified by:
      contains in interface Hitbox2D
      Parameters:
      point - the point to test
      Returns:
      whether the point is contained by this hitbox

    • createArea

      public Optional<Area> createArea()
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox, if it's finite, otherwise returns an empty optional.
      Specified by:
      createArea in interface Hitbox2D
      Returns:
      a new area of this hitbox, if applicable
      See Also:
      • Hitbox2D.createArea(AffineTransform)

    • getBounds

      public Optional<Rectangle2D> getBounds()
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Returns a rectangle that completely encloses this hitbox, if any.

      Typically returns empty for infinite hitboxes.

      Specified by:
      getBounds in interface Hitbox2D
      Returns:
      the bounding box, or empty if undefined
      See Also:
      • Hitbox2D.getBounds(AffineTransform)

    • isFinite

      public boolean isFinite()
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Determines whether this hitbox represents a finite region.

      Example: Hitbox2D.always() returns false, while a rectangle returns true.

      Specified by:
      isFinite in interface Hitbox2D
      Returns:
      whether the hitbox is finite

    • not

      public Hitbox2D not()
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Creates a 2D hitbox that covers every point except those covered by this hitbox. In other words, returns a negation/complement of this hitbox.
      Specified by:
      not in interface Hitbox2D
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Hitbox2D other)
      Description copied from interface: Hitbox2D
      Returns whether this hitbox and the given one cover exactly the same points.

      This method may return false negative results. False positives must not occur.

      Specified by:
      equals in interface Hitbox2D
      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox
      Returns:
      whether the two hitboxes are equivalent

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      obj - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the obj argument; false otherwise.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • children

      public Collection<? extends Hitbox2D> children()
      Returns the value of the children record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the children record component
